children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Administrator of the Transportation Security Administration (referred to in this section as “Administrator”) shall establish within the Transportation Security Administration the Surface Transportation Security Advisory Committee (referred to in this section as the “Advisory Committee”).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Advisory Committee may advise, consult with, report to, and make recommendations to the Administrator on surface transportation security matters, including the development, refinement, and implementation of policies, programs, initiatives, rulemakings, and security directives pertaining to surface transportation security.

children.heading: Risk-based security
children.label: 2 Risk-based security
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall consider risk-based security approaches in the performance of its duties.

children.label: A
children.text: voting members appointed by the Administrator under paragraph (2); and

children.label: i
children.text: the Transportation Security Administration;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Department of Transportation;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Coast Guard; and

children.label: iv
children.text: such other Federal department or agency as the Administrator considers appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: associations representing such modes of surface transportation;

children.label: B
children.text: labor organizations representing such modes of surface transportation;

children.label: C
children.text: groups representing the users of such modes of surface transportation, including asset manufacturers, as appropriate;

children.label: D
children.text: relevant law enforcement, first responders, and security experts; and

children.label: E
children.text: such other groups as the Administrator considers appropriate.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 3 Chairperson
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall select a chairperson from among its voting members.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term of each voting member of the Advisory Committee shall be 2 years, but a voting member may continue to serve until the Administrator appoints a successor.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: ii Reappointment
children.text: A voting member of the Advisory Committee may be reappointed.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Administrator may review the participation of a member of the Advisory Committee and remove such member for cause at any time.

children.heading: Access to information
children.label: ii Access to information
children.text: The Administrator may remove any member of the Advisory Committee that the Administrator determines should be restricted from reviewing, discussing, or possessing classified information or sensitive security information.

children.heading: Prohibition on compensation
children.label: 5 Prohibition on compensation
children.text: The members of the Advisory Committee shall not receive any compensation from the Government by reason of their service on the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administrator shall require the Advisory Committee to meet at least semiannually in person or through web conferencing and may convene additional meetings as necessary.

children.label: i
children.text: announced in the Federal Register;

children.label: ii
children.text: announced on a public website; and

children.label: iii
children.text: open to the public.

children.heading: Attendance
children.label: C Attendance
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall maintain a record of the persons present at each meeting.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Unless otherwise prohibited by other Federal law, minutes of the meetings shall be published on the public website under subsection (e)(5).

children.heading: Protection of classified and sensitive information
children.label: ii Protection of classified and sensitive information
children.text: The Advisory Committee may redact or summarize, as necessary, minutes of the meetings to protect classified or other sensitive information in accordance with law.

children.heading: Voting member access to classified and sensitive security information
children.label: 7 Voting member access to classified and sensitive security information

children.heading: Determinations
children.label: A Determinations
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date on which a voting member is appointed to the Advisory Committee and before that voting member may be granted any access to classified information or sensitive security information, the Administrator shall determine if the voting member should be restricted from reviewing, discussing, or possessing classified information or sensitive security information.

children.heading: Sensitive security information
children.label: i Sensitive security information
children.text: If a voting member is not restricted from reviewing, discussing, or possessing sensitive security information under subparagraph (A) and voluntarily signs a nondisclosure agreement, the voting member may be granted access to sensitive security information that is relevant to the voting member’s service on the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Classified information
children.label: ii Classified information
children.text: Access to classified materials shall be managed in accordance with Executive Order 13526 of (75 Fed. Reg. 707), or any subsequent corresponding Executive order.December 29, 2009

children.heading: Sensitive security information
children.label: i Sensitive security information
children.text: Voting members shall protect sensitive security information in accordance with part 1520 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.heading: Classified information
children.label: ii Classified information
children.text: Voting members shall protect classified information in accordance with the applicable requirements for the particular level of classification.

children.label: A
children.text: Aviation Security Advisory Committee established under .section 44946 of title 49

children.label: B
children.text: Maritime Security Advisory Committee established under .section 70112 of title 46

children.label: C
children.text: Railroad Safety Advisory Committee established by the Federal Railroad Administration.

children.heading: Subject matter experts
children.label: 9 Subject matter experts
children.text: The Advisory Committee may request the assistance of subject matter experts with expertise related to the jurisdiction of the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Periodic reports
children.label: 1 Periodic reports
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall periodically submit reports to the Administrator on matters requested by the Administrator or by a majority of the members of the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Submission
children.label: A Submission
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall submit to the Administrator and the appropriate congressional committees an annual report that provides information on the activities, findings, and recommendations of the Advisory Committee during the preceding year.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: B Publication
children.text: Not later than 6 months after the date that the Administrator receives an annual report under subparagraph (A), the Administrator shall publish a public version of the report, in accordance with .section 552a(b) of title 5

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: 1 Consideration
children.text: The Administrator shall consider the information, advice, and recommendations of the Advisory Committee in formulating policies, programs, initiatives, rulemakings, and security directives pertaining to surface transportation security.

children.label: A
children.text: if the Administrator agrees with the recommendation, a plan describing the actions that the Administrator has taken, will take, or recommends that the head of another Federal department or agency take to implement the recommendation; or

children.label: B
children.text: if the Administrator disagrees with the recommendation, a justification for that determination.

children.label: A
children.text: notify the appropriate congressional committees of the feedback, including the determination under subparagraph (A) or subparagraph (B) of that paragraph, as applicable; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide the appropriate congressional committees with a briefing upon request.

children.heading: Updates
children.label: 4 Updates
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date the Administrator receives a recommendation from the Advisory Committee under subsection (d)(2) that the Administrator agrees with, and quarterly thereafter until the recommendation is fully implemented, the Administrator shall submit a report to the appropriate congressional committees or post on the public website under paragraph (5) an update on the status of the recommendation.

children.label: A
children.text: lists the members of the Advisory Committee; and

children.label: B
children.text: provides the contact information for the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Nonapplicability of FACA
children.label: f Nonapplicability of FACA
children.text: The Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) shall not apply to the Advisory Committee or any subcommittee established under this section.1See References in Text note below.1
